qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Share token authentication bypass permits unauthorized CLI command execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes an authentication bypass in the HTTP/WebSocket interface that allows command execution via CLI mode. The advisory describes an SFTP root escape via prefix-based path validation in the SFTP subsystem. These are different vulnerable components (HTTP/WebSocket vs SFTP), different root causes (token-based auth bypass vs string-prefix path validation), and different attack surfaces. While both affect goshs, they are distinct vulnerabilities.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory (GHSA-5h6h-7rc9-3824) describes an SFTP root escape vulnerability in goshs caused by unsafe prefix-based path validation in the SFTP subsystem (sftpserver/helper.go:47-59). The vulnerable logic uses strings.HasPrefix for path validation, allowing authenticated SFTP users to escape the configured root and access sibling directories. The only qualifying finding (goshs-token-auth-bypass-cli-rce) describes a different vulnerability in the same repository (authentication bypass in HTTP/WebSocket interface), not the SFTP path traversal issue.
evidence: Advisory root cause: strings.HasPrefix check in sanitizePath() at sftpserver/helper.go:47-59 allows path traversal to sibling directories
Advisory attack surface: SFTP subsystem (sftpserver/sftpserver.go, sftpserver/handler.go, sftpserver/helper.go)
Advisory impact: Authenticated SFTP users can read/write files outside configured root
Finding (goshs-token-auth-bypass-cli-rce): HTTP/WebSocket authentication bypass, different component and root cause
